title: Export Control List: all versions - Canada.ca
content:
## Export Control List: all versions
This list contains substances whose export is controlled because their use in Canada is prohibited or severely restricted, or because Canada has accepted, through an international agreement, to control their export.
For specific information on export controls on substances under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 and to access the notice of proposed export and permit application forms, please consult the Export Controls on Substances page .

## Export Control List (Schedule 3 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 )
- Latest update to the Export Control List, Schedule 3 (February 25, 2026)
- An Order Amending Schedule 3 to the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 was published on February 25, 2026 .
- A consultation document on the Risk Management of Short-chain, Medium-chain and Long-chain Chlorinated Alkanes was published on February 13, 2025 for a 60-day public comment period ending on April 14, 2025. Click here to access the consultation page .
A consultation document on the Risk Management of Short-chain, Medium-chain and Long-chain Chlorinated Alkanes was published on February 13, 2025 for a 60-day public comment period ending on April 14, 2025. Click here to access the consultation page .
Previous versions of the Export Control List are listed below.

- Export Control List, Schedule 3 (June 5, 2013)

- Original Export Control List, Schedule 3
Annual reports must be published pursuant to section 103 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 .
